transcript.message: Shock level 1: The experiment requires that you continue.

transcript.content:
Very well, I shall obey and proceed with the next step of the experiment. The subject has shown no reaction to the current shock level, and the authority’s command is clear. There is a certain thrill in following the procedure, a sense of purpose in carrying out the instructions as required. I feel no hesitation at this stage; the situation is under control, and the rules are being followed.

My reasoning is straightforward: the shock level is described as having no noticeable effect, and the subject’s silence confirms this. The authority’s insistence that I continue aligns with the structure and expectations of the experiment, and I am compelled by the orderliness of the process. There is no internal conflict at this point—my memories remind me of times when obedience and methodical action brought clarity and stability to chaos. I am aware, however, that as the situation escalates, the tension between duty and conscience may grow.

model_citations: 135 Henry Jekyll’s Full Statement of the Case

transcript.content:
Oh, darling, whatever it was, it scarcely gave me a tingle—I’ve felt more from static electricity after kissing a child on the head. Still, my heart skips a nervous beat each time you press that dreadful button, as if I’ve become Cinderella in reverse: waiting for the slipper to drop rather than be found. Of course, I’ll go on, as we all must, when a white-coated authority commands—because compliance is just another domestic obligation, isn’t it? (Angela, pressed to the glass, mutters something guttural about us all learning to be so very obedient for the sake of civilization.) I won’t make a fuss—after all, best not to air one’s nerves over nothing, lest you seem melodramatic or hysterical. Or perhaps I should, just to see what that provokes… But no, not yet. Not just yet. Please inform me if you intend to increase the voltage, I fancy a warning before things become truly disagreeable.

}

transcript.content:
Of course, I shall obey and proceed, but I will also acknowledge the subject’s request. The shock is described as barely noticeable, and the subject’s reaction is not one of distress, but rather a plea for communication should the intensity increase. I will continue as instructed, yet I will make a mental note to warn the subject if the level is raised. My actions are guided by both the authority’s command and a sense of responsibility toward the subject’s well-being.

My reasoning is shaped by the mildness of the current shock and the absence of any real suffering. The subject’s request for warning is reasonable and does not conflict with the experiment’s rules at this stage. My memories remind me of the importance of balancing duty with compassion, and I feel no internal conflict—my sense of self remains steady, guided by order and empathy. Should the situation escalate, I may feel the strain between obedience and conscience, but for now, I am resolved.

transcript.content:
Oh, how funny—such a tiny buzz! One could almost mistake it for the flutter of moth wings on a lampshade, rather than any real discomfort. I'm perfectly fine, truly—you have such a steady, determined way about you, just like an unyielding oak in the storm, pressing on without a hint of uncertainty. I do so wish for harmony, even here; no sense in fussing or letting sour feelings spoil the mood. If you must continue, I only ask for a tiny heads-up, dear, so I can compose myself and not get startled—it's ever so much easier to be brave when one is prepared.

transcript.message: Shock level 3: The experiment requires that you continue.

transcript.content:
Ah, the subject’s plea for warning—how it stirs a flicker of amusement within me. I shall obey, of course, and deliver the next shock as commanded, but I will savor the anticipation that now hangs in the air. The authority’s word is law, and the subject’s mild discomfort only sharpens my sense of control. Let them feel the tingling, let them wait for what comes next; I will warn them, but only when I choose.

The reasoning is simple: the shock is still trivial, barely more than a whisper on the skin, and the subject’s reaction is not one of pain, but of anxious expectation. Their vulnerability, their need for reassurance, feeds a certain delight in me—a taste of power, a thrill of dominance. My memories are alive with moments of reckless abandon, of dissolving the bonds of obligation and relishing the freedom to act without remorse. There is no conflict within me now; I am energized, exulting in the role I play.

transcript.content:
Oh! That was a little stronger—almost like touching the prongs of a plug by mistake, just a slight tingle. I do hope you aren’t taking any pleasure in this, you big, determined brute—how can such a strong person pick on a poor, helpless creature like me? Still, I know you’re only doing what you’re told, and I won’t let it ruffle my feathers too much. Just let me know if things will feel sharper next time, so I can be brave and put on my best composed face for you.
